notes: a Optimistic opening assembly (at Gilgal?) (1:1-2) o Tribal conquests begin o Obedient Israelites initiate positive communication with Jehovah o Tribes set out to take land, with Jehovah's blessing and direction b failure of the tribes to take their lands (1:3-36) (1) Judah: more positive (2) Benjamin: negative (3) Joseph: negative (4) Zebuiun: negative (5) Asher: negative (6) Naphtali: negative (7) Dan: very negative a' disheartening closing assembly at Bokim (2:1-5) o Conquest ends in failure and divine condemnation o Jehovah initiates negative communication with disobedient Israelites o Jehovah will no longer help tribes take their land adapted from David A. Dorsey, The Literary Structure of the Old Testament

Judges is the Book of Sin. Judges is also a book of Punishments to purify them every time they sin... 13 times! Judges is also a book of Repentance... yes, every time they end up with repentance, and prying to God, and trusting in Him, and confessing their sins Judges is also the book of Deliverance... again and again, 13 times... the deliverance is by the Lord, but always using a human being, a Liberator Judges is also the book of Deliverance... again and again, 13 times... the deliverance is by the Lord, but always using a human being, a Liberator
Adoni-Bezek =" my lord is Besek" king of the Canaanite city of Bezek, killed by Israelites
Kirjath-arba =" city of Arba" early name of the city which after the conquest was called ‘Hebron’
Talmai =" furrowed" one of the 3 sons of the giant Anak who were slain by the men of Judah
Sheshai =" noble" son of Anak, one of the giants in the land of Canaan
Debir =" sanctuary" a town in the mountains of Judah west of Hebron and given to the priests and a city of refuge
Kirjath-sepher or Kirjath-sannah =" city of the book" a town in the mountains of Judah to the west of Hebron
with the children of Judah into the wilderness of Judah, which [lieth] in the south of Arad; and they went and dwelt among the people.
Arad =" a wild ass" a royal city of the Canaanites north of the wilderness of Judah
Hormah =" devotion" a town of the Canaanites, conquered by Joshua, allotted to Judah, and located in the south of Judah
Zephath =" watch-tower" a Canaanite city in Simeon renamed Hormah by the Israelites
Ekron =" emigration" or "torn up by the roots" the most northerly of the 5 principal cities of the Philistines; located in the lowlands of Judah and later given to Dan
Askelon or Ashkelon =" the fire of infamy: I shall be weighed" a maritime city of the Philistines, southwest of Jerusalem
descry (Heb. tuwr) to seek, search out, spy out, explore

quotes.text: Bethel =" house of God" ancient place and seat of worship in Ephraim on border of Benjamin, identified with Luz

notes: Taanach or Tanach =" sandy" an ancient Canaanite city conquered by Joshua and allotted to the half tribe of Manasseh although in the territory of Issachar; given to the Kohathite Levites; located on the west of the Jordan and near the waters of Megiddo

Megiddo or Megiddon =" place of crowds" ancient city of Canaan assigned to Manasseh and located on the southern rim of the plain of Esdraelon 6 miles (10 km) from Mount Carmel and 11 miles (18 km) from Nazareth
Ibleam =" devouring the people" a city of Manasseh apparently located in the territory of either Issachar or Asher
Beth-shean or Beth-Shan =" house of ease" a place in Manasseh, west of the Jordan
Gazer or Gezer =" portion" a Levitical city on the border of Ephraim
Nahalal or Nahallal or Nahalol =" pasture" one of the cities of Zebulun given to the Merarite Levites
the Canaanites dwelt among them, and became tributaries.
Rehob =" broad place" 1) the northern limit of the exploration of the spies in Canaan; on the road to Hamath 2) a town in Asher 3) a second town by the same name also in Asher
Helbah =" fertile" a town of Asher, probably on the plain of Phoenicia not far from Sidon
Accho =" his straitness" a seaport town in Asher north of Mount Carmel and south of Tyre
Sidon =" hunting" ancient Phoenician city, on Mediterranean coast north of Tyre
Beth-shemesh =" house of the sun" or "sun-temple" a town in southwest Judah
Heres =" the sun" a mountain inhabited by Amorites in Moab; the place where Gideon turned back from chasing the Midianites
Shaalbim or Shaalabbin =" place of foxes" a town in Dan occupied by the Amorites
Ajalon or Aijalon =" field of deer" a city of Zebulun, site unknown
Maaleh-acrabbim =" ascent of scorpions" a pass on the southern border of Palestine
